text.title: Fixed Vs Hourly Decision Framework Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding the fixed-vs-hourly decision. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: All 4 signals (scope_clarity, change_rate, client_maturity, domain_familiarity) MUST be scored on a 1-3 band scale (low/medium/high). Missing signals default to "low" until evidence is supplied.

rationale.source: Freelance pricing audit; Hourly Billing Is Nuts (Jonathan Stark, 2018)

rule.rationale: Unscored signals let bias drive the shape — usually toward "hourly because I'm scared".

rule.statement: Each option (fixed / hourly / hybrid) MUST carry numeric kill-criteria (e.g. fixed: change-rate exceeds X requested-changes/week, reopen as hybrid). "We'll know when we see it" is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without numeric kill-criteria, the chosen option becomes default forever.

rule.statement: The decision record MUST contain: option chosen, options rejected, evidence cited per signal, reversal trigger. The reversal trigger is non-optional.
rule.rationale: Undocumented reversals erode trust faster than wrong decisions.

rule.statement: The record MUST be signed by ONE named decider (handle / role with handle). Anonymous "team chose" is rejected.
At 25% of project elapsed, the decider MUST reassess: are the original signals still true? If any signal flipped band, file a delta and consider re-shape.
rule.rationale: Scope drift is detected late; mid-project check forces early reshape rather than end-of-project surprise.
